subject: Chemistry
title: Alcohol and Perfume Distillation
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Alcohol and Perfume Distillation.

hook.text: Alchemists seeking the elixir of life kept distilling wine until they got pure ethanol—'aqua vitae,' the water of life.

buildup.text: Wine is only 12–15% alcohol. Distillation concentrates it by boiling off ethanol (78°C) before water (100°C).

discovery.text: By the 12th century, European alchemists produced 'spirits'—concentrated alcohol used as medicine and solvent.

twist.text: Islamic alchemists also distilled flowers to create pure essential oils. This created the perfume industry.

climax.text: Rose water from distillation became a luxury product. Damascus was the perfume capital of the medieval world.

punchline.text: Seeking eternal life, they found spirits and perfume. Immortality in a bottle—sort of.

quiz.question: What does 'aqua vitae' mean, and what is it?
